summary: TypeScript code (matrix.ts, cli.ts) is solid: explicit types, proper validation, custom error class, clear algorithm comments. Workflow has critical gaps: (1) requirement 'The act-result.txt file MUST exist when done' is unmet—workflow never creates or writes this artifact; (2) requirement 'Run actionlint...and fix errors' is unmet—no actionlint validation step in workflow; (3) fixture files (fixtures/basic.json, etc.) are referenced but not shown/created, workflow will fail without them; (4) test cases 1–3 repeat nearly identical run→jq→validate patterns (e.g., lines 37–40, 43–52, 55–64) without factoring into helper/composite action. Workflow structure is sound (pinned actions, minimal permissions, proper job deps, `set -e` in bash), but deliverable cannot run standalone with `act push --rm` as specified.
